template.description: EnergyID Webhook flow: device provisioning via /hello and periodic sensor data upload. Configure provisioning_key, provisioning_secret, device_id, device_name as NiFi Variables on the Process Group.
template.name: EnergyID Webhook

processGroups.comments: EnergyID Webhook integration. Set Variables: provisioning_key, provisioning_secret, device_id, device_name.
processGroups.name: EnergyID Webhook

config.comments: Triggers /hello authentication every 24 hours. Run manually or on schedule.
Builds the JSON body for POST /hello
POST to https://hooks.energyid.eu/hello with provisioning credentials
Extracts webhookUrl, authorization, x-twin-id, and uploadInterval from /hello JSON response
Copies extracted auth attributes into stable attribute names used by the data-upload branch. Also records the auth timestamp.
Logs the auth result attributes for debugging. Safe to auto-terminate in production.
Triggers sensor data upload on a schedule matching the uploadInterval returned by /hello (default 5 min). In a real integration replace this with a processor that reads actual sensor values.
Builds the JSON payload. ts is the current Unix timestamp in seconds (mandatory). el is the electricity reading in kWh. Replace 12345.67 with a real attribute reference such as ${sensor.value}.

POSTs sensor data JSON to the webhookUrl obtained during /hello authentication. Authorization and x-twin-id headers are read from FlowFile attributes set by the auth branch. IMPORTANT: in a real multi-node setup these attributes must be propagated via a distributed cache (e.g., DistributedMapCache) rather than relying on FlowFile attributes from a separate branch.
Routes the response by HTTP status code. 200/201 → success. 401 → token expired, trigger re-auth. 429 → rate-limited. All others → failure.
Logs successful data upload. Auto-terminates the FlowFile after logging.

Logs a 401 Unauthorized response. Connect the 'success' output of this processor back to the 'Build /hello Body' ReplaceText processor (or to the auth GenerateFlowFile) to trigger re-authentication. In this template it auto-terminates; wire the connection manually after import to close the re-auth loop.

Logs a 429 Too Many Requests response. The Retry-After header value is available in the invokehttp.response.headers attribute. Add a Wait/Notify processor pair or a penalise connection to implement back-off.
Logs any unexpected HTTP status codes (4xx other than 401/429, 5xx, etc.).
